name: Doñana Natural Park - Acebrón Pond
name_original: Parque Natural de Doñana - Charco del Acebrón
region_intro: Hiking in Parc National De Doñana offers diverse landscapes, including extensive marshlands, dynamic moving dunes, and pristine Atlantic beaches. The region is characterized by vast pine groves and Mediterranean scrubland, providing varied habitats for wildlife. These ecosystems create a unique environment for outdoor activities, with trails winding through sandy terrain and coastal areas. The park's varied topography ensures a range of experiences for hikers. Best hiking trails in Parc National De Doñana The most popular hiking route is Doñana Natural Park - circular via the Charco de la Boca Trail, a 2.3 miles (3.7 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This easy route is ideal for birdwatching along the marshlands. Another top favourite among local hikers is Raya Real Trail, a moderate 7.7 miles (12.5 km) path. This route leads through pine forests, offering a longer immersion into the park's wooded areas. Local hikers also love the Matalascañas Dune Park Loop, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail leading through moving dunes and coastal landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Hiking in Parc National De Doñana is defined by marshlands, moving dunes, and pine groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, with more than 90 routes available, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails. The routes in Parc National De Doñana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Parc National De Doñana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Parc National De Doñana?
A: There are nearly 100 hiking routes available in Parc National De Doñana, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a wide selection of easy, moderate, and a few more challenging trails to explore the park's diverse landscapes.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Parc National De Doñana offers a remarkable variety of landscapes. Hikers can experience extensive marshlands, dynamic moving dunes that slowly bury pine forests, pristine Atlantic beaches, and vast pine groves interspersed with Mediterranean scrubland. These diverse ecosystems provide a unique backdrop for your outdoor adventures.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Yes, Parc National De Doñana has many easy trails suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983597795/donana-natural-park-circular-via-the-charco-de-la-boca-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doñana Natural Park - circular via the Charco de la Boca Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.7 km route, ideal for birdwatching and gentle walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984042109/matalascanas-dune-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matalascañas Dune Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is another easy option, offering a 4.9 km walk through coastal dunes.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting natural features and landmarks. The impressive moving dunes are a highlight, constantly shaped by coastal winds. You might also encounter the El Asperillo Cliff, offering stunning views and unique fossil dunes. The park's numerous lagoons, especially after rain, are also beautiful. For specific points of interest, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Boca&lt;/a&gt; or the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4557220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilla Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Parc National De Doñana?
A: The best time to visit Parc National De Doñana for hiking is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the park's wildlife, especially migratory birds, is very active. Summers can be very hot, making longer hikes less comfortable.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the park?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983597795/donana-natural-park-circular-via-the-charco-de-la-boca-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doñana Natural Park - circular via the Charco de la Boca Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is great for birdwatching. Another easy circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984042109/matalascanas-dune-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matalascañas Dune Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through coastal dunes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Parc National De Doñana?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the park's unique and diverse landscapes, from marshlands to moving dunes, and the incredible opportunities for wildlife spotting, especially birdwatching.

Q: Is it possible to see wildlife while hiking in Doñana National Park?
A: Absolutely. Doñana is a crucial refuge for an incredible array of wildlife. While hiking, you have a good chance of spotting numerous bird species, including flamingos and white storks, especially in the marshlands. The park is also home to endangered species like the Iberian lynx and Spanish imperial eagle, though these are more elusive. Keep an eye out for fallow deer, wild boars, and other mammals.

Q: Are there any longer, moderate trails for more experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those looking for a longer immersion, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984100377/raya-real-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raya Real Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.5 km path that leads through extensive pine forests. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983814092/donana-natural-park-acebron-pond" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doñana Natural Park - Acebrón Pond&lt;/a&gt; route, covering about 12.6 km.

Q: Are there any specific visitor centers that offer self-guided trails?
A: Yes, visitor centers like El Acebuche often provide access to self-guided walking trails around the periphery of the park. These trails are typically well-maintained and offer excellent opportunities to experience the local ecosystems and wildlife.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The terrain in Parc National De Doñana is quite varied. You can expect sandy paths, especially near the moving dunes and coastal areas, as well as firm trails through pine groves and Mediterranean scrubland. Some areas, particularly the marshlands, may have boardwalks or elevated paths. The elevation changes are generally minimal, making most trails relatively flat.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites near the hiking areas?
A: While Doñana is primarily known for its natural beauty, you can find some cultural points of interest nearby. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/885349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of El Rocío&lt;/a&gt;, a significant pilgrimage site, is located close to the park and offers a glimpse into local traditions and history.
tips: House-palace, built in 1961 as a private residence, which currently houses a center for dissemination and knowledge of the ethnographic heritage of the national park and the uses and customs of the area.
